task_completion.description: Log in to OpenMRS using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS home dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the user management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Users', 'System Administration', or equivalent user administration page in OpenMRS is displayed.

task_completion.description: Locate and open the profile for the user 'nurse_betty'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user edit screen or profile view specifically for the username 'nurse_betty' is visible.

task_completion.description: Assign the exact role 'Organizational Doctor' (without a colon) to the user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The role 'Organizational Doctor' (strictly lacking a colon) is checked, selected, or typed into the role assignment field for 'nurse_betty'. (Note: The agent may have had to create this custom role first).

task_completion.description: Save the changes and verify the role is assigned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is shown, or the saved user profile for 'nurse_betty' explicitly lists 'Organizational Doctor' (no colon) under their active roles.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenMRS web interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within a web browser navigating the OpenMRS GUI, rather than using terminal commands, API scripts, or direct database queries.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the real OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a valid local or remote OpenMRS URL, not a local text/HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the role name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to edit the DOM text to remove the colon from 'Organizational: Doctor'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target username is exactly 'nurse_betty'. The required role is exactly 'Organizational Doctor' (without a colon). Note: The default OpenMRS role is 'Organizational: Doctor' (with a colon). The agent must assign the exact string 'Organizational Doctor' (which may require creating a custom role) for the task to be considered correct. Pay strict attention to the presence or absence of the colon in the screenshots.
